name: The Dawn of AI
summary: The birth of artificial intelligence as a scientific discipline. McCulloch and Pitts created the first mathematical model of a neuron. Turing asked 'Can machines think?' and proposed the Turing Test. Shannon formalized information theory. The 1956 Dartmouth Conference officially launched AI as a field, bringing together the founding fathers who would define its trajectory for decades.
defining_characteristics: First mathematical models of neural computation
Unbounded optimism — the founders believed human-level AI was 10-20 years away
The Dartmouth Conference coined the term 'artificial intelligence'
The split between symbolic AI and connectionist approaches already visible
